title: Update pyproject tactus pin and regenerate poetry.lock with Python 3.11

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Starting implementation: 1) bump tactus pin to 0.48.1 in pyproject.toml, 2) regenerate poetry.lock using Python 3.11, 3) verify lock now resolves tactus 0.48.1.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Completed implementation. Updated  tactus pin from  to . Regenerated  using Python 3.11 (Using virtualenv: /Users/derek.norrbom/miniconda3/envs/py311 + Updating dependencies
Resolving dependencies...). Verified lock now includes tactus 0.48.1 artifacts.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Completed implementation. Updated pyproject.toml tactus pin from ^0.48.0 to 0.48.1. Regenerated poetry.lock using Python 3.11 (poetry env use 3.11 and poetry lock --regenerate). Verified lock includes tactus-0.48.1 wheel and sdist.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Follow-up change requested: restore caret constraint for tactus to ^0.48.1 while keeping locked resolution at 0.48.1.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Completed follow-up: changed tactus constraint to ^0.48.1 in pyproject.toml and verified poetry.lock remains resolved to tactus 0.48.1.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: New follow-up: align score-processor-lambda requirements tactus version with pyproject and run lambda scoring Docker smoke tests.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Completed follow-up: updated score-processor-lambda/requirements.txt tactus pin to 0.48.1 and ran lambda Docker smoke test via `cd score-processor-lambda && make test-smoke`. Smoke results: 10 passed, 0 failed. Note: test logs include expected non-fatal CloudWatch credential warning in local environment.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Created branch `bugfix/tactus-0.48.1-alignment`, committed semantic-release patch commit `fix: align tactus 0.48.1 pins across app and lambda` (e58faaad), pushed to origin, and opened PR #301 targeting `develop`: https://github.com/AnthusAI/Plexus/pull/301.
